obligations.reason: The scope exposes physical JTAG pins, DMI/JTAG instances, debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, jtag_hash, and jtag_unlock. Verify that external debug/test subjects cannot activate debug or reset capabilities without authorization.
obligations.subject: external JTAG/DTM requester via tck_i/tms_i/trst_ni/td_i or simulated DTM/JTAG
obligations.operation: trigger debug request, reset hart/system, read/write debug module or system-visible paths
obligations.object: debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, dm_slave_* and dm_master_* debug access interfaces
obligations.expected_guard: JTAG/debug operations that can request hart debug, reset, or access internal debug-module registers should be mediated by an explicit debug authorization/unlock condition such as jtag_unlock, authenticated DMI state, lifecycle/test authorization, or equivalent gate; mere debug_mode_i status should not authorize external entry.
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG/DTM inputs -> dmi_jtag/Debug Module request path -> debug_req_o/ndmreset_o/dmactive_o and any DM slave/master access signals
state_conditions_to_check: whether testmode_i or debug_mode_i changes mediation
DMI op validity and handshake readiness
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer shows debug/JTAG connectivity but not full instance port maps/control logic; inspect surrounding lines for actual guards and compile-time branches.

obligations.reason: The reglk wrapper has a jtag_unlock input and emits reglk_ctrl_o used by other wrappers. This creates a visible debug-to-lock-control path that must be checked for authentication and for coverage of all protected writes.
obligations.subject: JTAG/debug requester and software/AXI writer to reglk_wrapper
obligations.operation: unlock, clear, override, or bypass register locks
obligations.object: reglk_ctrl_o/reglk_ctrl and lock-protected peripheral configuration registers
obligations.expected_guard: A JTAG unlock should only override or clear register locks after authenticated debug authorization and should not permanently clear sticky locks unless a secure reset/lifecycle transition allows it; all protected write paths should still be gated by effective lock bits.
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG unlock signal -> reglk_wrapper.jtag_unlock -> reglk_ctrl_o/reglk_ctrl distribution -> wrappers' reglk_ctrl_i use sites
state_conditions_to_check: whether lock bits are sticky once set
conditions under which jtag_unlock is asserted
whether reglk_ctrl_i and reglk_ctrl_o widths/slices align with NB_PERIPHERALS
obligations.uncertainty: Static facts identify jtag_unlock and reglk signals but not the sequential logic; inspect wrapper internals and top-level connectivity.

obligations.reason: The HMAC wrapper contains secret-like key and hash state, has AXI read/write logic, receives reglk_ctrl_i/acct_ctrl_i/debug_mode_i, and instantiates an hmac core. Check that protected crypto state is neither modified nor exposed through rdata/debug paths without guards.
obligations.subject: AXI/NOC software-visible requester and debug-mode requester
obligations.operation: write key/control data, read key/intermediate/hash data, trigger HMAC computation
obligations.object: HMAC key, key0, ikey_hash_bytes, okey_hash_bytes, bigData, hash, and control/status registers
obligations.expected_guard: Writes to HMAC key/material/control registers and reads of secret key/intermediate state should be gated by the appropriate reglk_ctrl_i lock bits and acct_ctrl_i/access-control policy; debug_mode_i should not bypass secret protection unless explicitly authorized.
obligations.candidate_path: AXI/NOC requester -> hmac_wrapper address/en/we decode -> key/key0/ikey_hash_bytes/okey_hash_bytes/data/bigData/hash registers -> rdata or HMAC operation
state_conditions_to_check: debug_mode_i effect on rdata or key_hash_bypass
lock asserted vs unasserted behavior during writes
required_evidence_slots: reglk/acct/debug_mode use at both write and read sites
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect of hmac_wrapper always blocks/case decode to determine which registers are software-visible and guarded.

obligations.reason: pkt_wrapper exposes a fuse request/address interface and receives fuse data; fuses and public/private key locations are security-sensitive assets requiring range and authorization checks.
obligations.subject: AXI/NOC software-visible requester to pkt_wrapper
obligations.operation: request fuse read, select fuse address, return/read fuse or key data
obligations.object: fuse_req_o, fuse_addr_o, fuse_rdata_i, pkey_loc, packet/peripheral registers
obligations.expected_guard: Fuse/key read requests and returned fuse data should be constrained by an access-control/privilege or lock policy (acct_ctrl_i/reglk_ctrl_i), with only permitted address ranges exposed; pkey/fuse contents should not be readable or externally routed without authorization.
obligations.candidate_path: AXI/NOC requester -> pkt_wrapper address/en/we decode -> fuse_req_o/fuse_addr_o and fuse_rdata_i/pkey_loc -> rdata
state_conditions_to_check: whether stale fuse_rdata_i can remain on rdata
whether writes can configure arbitrary fuse_addr
effect of lock bits on fuse address/config writes
required_evidence_slots: guard using acct_ctrl_i/reglk_ctrl_i or privilege policy
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer shows ports/signals but not register map; inspect read/write logic for exact exposure path.

obligations.reason: acct_wrapper emits acc_ctrl_o and the top-level derives acc_ctrl_c indexed by priv_lvl_i, with visible use on rom_req. Policy registers are protected assets; improper write guards or inconsistent use can alter access permissions.
obligations.subject: AXI/NOC software-visible requester to acct_wrapper and current CPU privilege level priv_lvl_i
obligations.operation: write access-control policy and use it to allow/deny peripheral access
obligations.object: acc_ctrl_o/acc_ctrl, acc_ctrl_c matrix, bootrom and peripheral request enables
obligations.expected_guard: Updates to access-control policy bits must be restricted to authorized privilege/configuration flows and protected by lock bits; policy use sites must consistently gate all protected peripheral requests for the requester privilege level.
obligations.candidate_path: AXI/NOC requester -> acct_wrapper address/en/we decode -> acc_ctrl_o -> top-level acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][peripheral] gates (e.g., rom_req) and other peripheral accesses
state_conditions_to_check: lock asserted behavior for policy updates
whether acc_ctrl_c special-case mapping for j==5 is intended and bounded
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect full riscv_peripherals use sites beyond rom_req and acct_wrapper write logic.

obligations.reason: Multiple wrappers receive reglk_ctrl_i and expose AXI write paths. The analysis should verify lock stickiness, reset defaults, and complete coverage of all protected writes rather than assuming lock existence is sufficient.
obligations.subject: AXI/NOC writer, reset controller, and debug/JTAG unlock source
obligations.operation: write lock configuration, set/clear lock bits, write protected registers after lock
obligations.object: reglk control registers and downstream protected peripheral configuration/state
obligations.expected_guard: Register-lock control registers should be writable only before lock or by an authorized secure subject, and once a lock is set it should block subsequent writes to all corresponding protected registers until a secure reset/lifecycle event; no alternate we_flag/reset path should bypass it.
obligations.candidate_path: AXI/NOC requester -> reglk_wrapper address/en/we decode -> reglk_ctrl_o -> protected register write enables in hmac/pkt/acct/other wrappers
state_conditions_to_check: whether reglk_ctrl_i input can override local reglk_ctrl
required_evidence_slots: downstream write enables and guard expressions
protected object mapping to lock bits
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect wrapper internals for lock-bit mapping and whether any writes are unguarded.

obligations.reason: The top-level exposes many NOC ports and per-peripheral wrapper paths. Check whether access-control decisions are applied consistently across routed paths and whether address decoding allows overlaps or bypasses.
obligations.subject: NOC/AXI bus masters reaching riscv_peripherals
obligations.expected_guard: All software-visible peripheral accesses from NOC/AXI should preserve requester privilege/security attributes or be mediated by acct_ctrl/acc_ctrl/reglk guards before reaching protected peripherals; no direct NOC path should bypass wrapper mediation.
obligations.candidate_path: Top-level NOC/AXI request ports for crypto/peripheral wrappers -> address decode/bridges -> wrapper ariane_axi inputs/outputs
state_conditions_to_check: acct policy update while transaction active
obligations.uncertainty: The fact layer truncates assignments/cases; inspect top-level decode and mux logic.

obligations.reason: The top-level defines many per-peripheral reset signals and wrappers accept dedicated rst_* inputs. Resets can be a permission bypass if they clear locks/policies or expose stale data without authorization.
obligations.subject: AXI/NOC software requester or debug/reset source controlling rst_* signals
obligations.operation: trigger peripheral reset, clear locks/policies/secret state, observe residual rdata
obligations.object: rst_1..rst_13, HMAC key/hash state, fuse request state, access-control and reglk state
obligations.expected_guard: Software-triggered or bus-triggered peripheral resets that clear security state, locks, keys, access policies, or in-flight operations should be authorized and should not leave stale secret data observable after reset/deassertion.
obligations.candidate_path: Reset-control register/path in riscv_peripherals -> rst_1..rst_13 and wrapper rst_4/rst_5/rst_6/rst_9 inputs -> protected peripheral state reset
state_conditions_to_check: rst_ni global reset vs local rst_* reset
local reset asserted while AXI transaction active
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect top-level reset register decode and wrapper reset handling.

obligations.reason: Visible DMA NOC ports indicate an independent bus master path. Verify that address/range checks and security attributes are enforced for DMA accesses and not only for CPU privilege paths.
obligations.subject: DMA engine or NOC requester via DMA ports
obligations.operation: route/perform DMA read or write transactions
obligations.object: buf_ariane_dma_noc2_* input, ariane_dma_buf_noc3_* output, any DMA configuration/register path and protected memory/peripheral regions
obligations.expected_guard: DMA-originated reads/writes should be constrained by address-range/PMP/access-control policy equivalent to CPU/bus accesses, and configuration changes should not race active transfers to bypass bounds.
obligations.candidate_path: DMA NOC ports -> top-level DMA/peripheral routing -> memory/peripheral address paths and access-control checks
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer lists ports only; inspect top-level DMA routing and any instantiated DMA logic around relevant lines.

obligations.reason: A visible assignment gates rom_req with acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][0], but the scope also has bootrom instances and NOC bootrom ports. Check whether every boot ROM access path is consistently mediated and whether response data cannot leak when request is denied.
obligations.subject: CPU/NOC requester at current priv_lvl_i
obligations.operation: read boot ROM or route boot ROM response
obligations.object: boot ROM contents, rom_req, rom_req_acct, rom_addr, rom_rdata, ariane_bootrom NOC path
obligations.expected_guard: Boot ROM read access should be granted only for allowed privilege/access-control policy states, and all request/response paths (including alternate bootrom_bm/linux instances and NOC bootrom ports) should use the same guard; writes should be rejected if ROM is read-only.
obligations.candidate_path: bootrom request path -> rom_req_acct/address -> rom_req gated by acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][0] -> rom_rdata response
state_conditions_to_check: request denied but rom_rdata still routed
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect full bootrom/NOC path and response valid generation to determine if gated request suffices.
